_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, representing an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices and indexed to 2015 as 100, currently stands at 141. This indicates a 41% increase since 2015. The rise has been uneven and driven by recent market volatility rather than a steady long-term trend. It reflects changes in specific commodity prices within the basket rather than general inflation in the food sector.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have nearly doubled since 2015, increasing by 99%, making it the largest contributor to the basket's rise. Orange juice prices rose by 34%, wheat by 19%, and sugar by 13%. These changes are primarily due to weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought affecting coffee-growing regions and citrus greening plus hurricanes hitting orange juice production areas. These soft commodity markets are thin, meaning limited supply can lead to sharp price swings.
notes.why: The headline increase is driven largely by a few weather-sensitive crops rather than broad inflationary pressures. Given the exposure of these commodities to climate and disease risks, elevated prices can persist for multiple years as supply adjustments take time.
